_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Subsidy Rationalisation Targets ~RM8 Billion Annual Savings; Delivery Mechanism Still Untested
context: The Anwar government targets approximately RM8 billion in annual savings from electricity, diesel, and the planned RON95 rationalisation. The PADU database (~30.4 million profiles) is the targeting backbone, but no independent audit of delivery accuracy has been published.

cards.text: Targeted subsidy reform aims to save ~RM8 billion a year, but no independent audit yet shows whether the targeting actually works.
cards.sub: Diesel rationalisation (June 2024) and electricity tariff restructuring contributed the bulk of savings to date. RON95 targeting begins in the second half of 2025.

cards.h: PADU (Pangkalan Data Utama) holds about 30.4 million individual profiles and is the official targeting backbone.
cards.s: Public reporting has flagged data-quality and security concerns since launch. Rafizi Ramli, the minister who built PADU, resigned over disagreements about how it would be used.
cards.d: PADU (Pangkalan Data Utama) holds about 30.4 million individual profiles and is the official targeting backbone. Public reporting has flagged data-quality and security concerns since launch. Rafizi Ramli, the minister who built PADU, resigned over disagreements about how it would be used.

cards.h: Verified savings figures so far come from electricity (~RM4B) and diesel (~RM4B). Headline 'rationalisation savings' should be read against these components.
cards.s: The Ministry of Finance has cited an ~RM8 billion annual savings target overall.
cards.d: Verified savings figures so far come from electricity (~RM4B) and diesel (~RM4B). Headline 'rationalisation savings' should be read against these components. The Ministry of Finance has cited an ~RM8 billion annual savings target overall.

cards.h: No independent body publishes audited results on whether targeting reaches the right households.
cards.s: Subsidy effectiveness reporting is still done by the implementing ministries themselves. Independent evaluation infrastructure remains the gap.
cards.d: No independent body publishes audited results on whether targeting reaches the right households. Subsidy effectiveness reporting is still done by the implementing ministries themselves. Independent evaluation infrastructure remains the gap.

cards.text: The story is no longer the savings number. It is whether targeting accuracy will ever be measured by anyone other than the ministries delivering it.
Like fixing a leaky pipe and announcing the savings before anyone has measured the new flow rate.
cards.sub: Headline savings mean little until delivery accuracy is independently audited.

cards.text: Subsidy rationalisation needs an independent audit body publishing results per cohort, not the implementing ministries grading themselves.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
